params.name: enterpriseAccountId
params.description: The unique identifier of the enterprise account (starts with 'ent').

params.name: include
params.description: Additional information to include in the response.

items.docs: Returns information about the specified enterprise account, including the enterprise name, creation time, and optionally aggregated usage data and descendant accounts.

params.name: enterpriseAccountId
params.description: The unique identifier of the enterprise account (starts with 'ent').

params.name: email
params.description: Filter by one or more email addresses.

params.name: id
params.description: Filter by one or more user IDs.

params.name: include
params.description: Additional information to include for each user.

items.docs: Returns a list of users within the enterprise account. Results can be filtered by user IDs or email addresses, and can include collaboration details.

params.name: enterpriseAccountId
params.description: The unique identifier of the enterprise account (starts with 'ent').

items.docs: Deletes one or more users from the enterprise account by their user IDs. This permanently removes the users and their data.

info.name: Airtable Remove a User from Enterprise

params.name: enterpriseAccountId
params.description: The unique identifier of the enterprise account (starts with 'ent').

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user to remove.

items.docs: Removes a user from the enterprise account. Optionally, a replacement user can be specified to take over shared resources. The response includes details about affected shared and unshared resources.

params.name: enterpriseAccountId
params.description: The unique identifier of the enterprise account (starts with 'ent').

items.docs: Claims one or more users into the enterprise account, setting their management status to either managed or unmanaged.

params.name: enterpriseAccountId
params.description: The unique identifier of the enterprise account (starts with 'ent').

items.docs: Updates the managed or unmanaged status for one or more users in the enterprise account.

info.name: Airtable Grant Admin Access to Users

params.name: enterpriseAccountId
params.description: The unique identifier of the enterprise account (starts with 'ent').

items.docs: Grants enterprise admin access to one or more users, allowing them to manage the enterprise account through the Admin panel and API.

info.name: Airtable Revoke Admin Access from Users

params.name: enterpriseAccountId
params.description: The unique identifier of the enterprise account (starts with 'ent').

items.docs: Revokes enterprise admin access from one or more users. The users retain their regular enterprise membership but lose admin privileges.

params.name: groupId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user group.

params.name: include
params.description: Additional information to include.

items.docs: Returns details about a specific user group, including its members and configuration.

info.name: Airtable Move User Groups Between Enterprises

params.name: enterpriseAccountId
params.description: The unique identifier of the enterprise account (starts with 'ent').

items.docs: Moves one or more user groups from this enterprise account to a target enterprise account.
Creates a new workspace within the enterprise account.

params.name: workspaceId
params.description: The unique identifier of the workspace (starts with 'wsp').

items.docs: Permanently deletes the specified workspace and all its contents. This action cannot be undone.

params.name: workspaceId
params.description: The unique identifier of the workspace.

params.name: include
params.description: Additional information to include in the response.

items.docs: Returns a list of all collaborators on the specified workspace, including individual users and user groups with their permission levels.

params.name: enterpriseAccountId
params.description: The unique identifier of the enterprise account (starts with 'ent').

items.docs: Moves one or more workspaces from this enterprise account to a target enterprise account.
